pi_items.source: Found explicitly in task.json description, setup_task.sh and export_result.sh.
Found explicitly in task.json description, setup_task.sh and export_result.sh.
Confirmed via setup_task.sh which explicitly copies simple_model_rocket.ork to this location.
Confirmed via verifier.py logic explicitly enforcing len(stages) >= 2.
Confirmed via verifier.py logic explicitly enforcing pass threshold of 65 points.

privileged_info_summary: The task relies on the real OpenRocket example 'A simple model rocket' designed by Sampo Niskanen. Analysis reveals a fatal flaw in verifier.py: it incorrectly counts `<stage>` tags nested inside `<motorconfiguration>` elements. Because the baseline single-stage rocket contains 5 motor configurations, the verifier sees 6 total stages, erroneously passing the multi-stage check (min_stages >= 2). Furthermore, the single-stage sustainer acts as the evaluated 'booster' and correctly triggers the body tube, trapezoidal fin set, and 'uptodate' simulation checks. Consequently, an agent can score 85/100 and pass the task (threshold 65) simply by copying the original `.ork` file to the target path without making any actual multi-stage modifications or writing the requested staging report.
